summary: Ohm's law links the voltage across a resistor, the current through it, and its resistance: U = I·R. It is the starting point for almost every circuit problem.
method: Identify the part of the circuit you are analysing and the two known quantities.
Decide which quantity is unknown — voltage, current, or resistance.
Rearrange U = I·R for the unknown (I = U/R or R = U/I).
Use SI units: volts, amperes, ohms.
Substitute and compute; sanity-check that a bigger resistance gives a smaller current.

example.title: Current through a resistor
steps: The unknown is I, so use I = U/R.
